title: Network Connectivity Troubleshooting
description: Triage an access-layer connectivity complaint: link down, packet loss, or slow throughput.
safety_notes: Confirm an approved change window before any disruptive action (port reset, reload).
Do not remove cables on a live trunk without confirming impact.

steps.title: Classify the symptom
steps.instruction: Ask the user what they're seeing. Is the link completely down (no connectivity), intermittently dropping, or up but slow?

branches.condition: No connectivity at all — link is down

steps.title: Check physical layer
steps.instruction: Check the port LED and interface status (admin vs operational). Reseat the cable and transceiver at both ends and try a known-good cable. Is the port now up?

branches.condition: Port came up after physical check

steps.title: Check port configuration
steps.instruction: Verify the port's admin state, speed/duplex, and VLAN/trunk config against the far end. Move the device to a known-good port to isolate port vs device. Did isolation point to the switch port?

branches.condition: The end device is at fault

steps.title: Inspect interface error counters
steps.instruction: Baseline the interface counters. Are CRC/input errors rising (cabling/optic/EMI) or output drops rising (congestion)?

steps.title: Check speed/duplex and utilization
steps.instruction: Confirm negotiated speed/duplex matches both ends (duplex mismatch causes slowness + CRCs). Check interface utilization. Is there a duplex mismatch?

branches.condition: Negotiation is correct; likely congestion or host-side

steps.title: Resolved — physical layer
steps.instruction: Link restored by reseating/replacing the cable or transceiver. Document the faulty component.

steps.title: Resolved — end device
steps.instruction: Fault isolated to the end device; hand back to the endpoint owner. Document the isolation steps.

steps.title: Resolved — cabling
steps.instruction: CRC/input errors indicate a cabling or optic problem. Replace the run/optic and re-baseline counters.

steps.title: Resolved — duplex
steps.instruction: Set both ends to matching speed/duplex (or auto/auto) and re-test. Document the change.

steps.title: Escalate / change-controlled fix
steps.instruction: The fix needs config change, congestion remediation, or is beyond first-line. Capture findings and escalate to network engineering within the change window.
